sourceMovie.title: What a Girl Wants
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: What a Girl Wants (2003) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Dennie Gordon's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Love Don't Co$t a Thing
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If What a Girl Wants clicked for you, Love Don't Co$t a Thing (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after What a Girl Wants?
faq.answer: Try Love Don't Co$t a Thing, Bend It Like Beckham, Hearts Beat Loud—each shares DNA with What a Girl Wants while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is What a Girl Wants?
faq.answer: What a Girl Wants (2003) is best described as Comedy, Romance, Drama, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is What a Girl Wants worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
